images.caption: Figure 1:Participation ratio (PR) of the token field: the RGB input field (32.8) is high-dimensional, while task-adapted fields collapse to compact subspaces (1.1–4.2), motivating a token-local linear readout rather than a generative decoder. Per-task PR values are illustrative; the linear head’s sufficiency is shown empirically in Sec.4.3.

Figure 2:ReChannel vs. target-side generation.Existing generative and editing interfaces treat dense targets as image-like outputs:
the target is encoded into an RGB-trained VAE latent, supervised there, and decoded back
through a target-side reconstruction path. ReChannel instead keeps the RGB input interface
but drops the target-side path: the pretrained backbone acts as an RGB-native field
organizer, and a lightweight linear readout maps its spatial tokens directly to
task-native pixel-space patches under pixel supervision—field readout rather than target
generation.
